results.code:
229 
230     def jwt_login(
231         self, name: str,
232         login_url: str,
233         username: str, password: str,
234         username_field: str = "email",
235         password_field: str = "password",
236         token_path: str = "token",           # dot-notation JSON path
237         content_type: str = "application/json",
238     ) -> AuthProfile:
239         """
240         Perform a login POST and extract JWT from JSON response.
241         Stores the token for subsequent requests.
242         """
243         log.info("JWT login: %s as %s", login_url, username)
244 
245         if content_type == "application/json":
246             payload = json.dumps({username_field: username, password_field: password}).encode()
247         else:
248             payload = urllib.parse.urlencode({username_field: username,
249                                               password_field: password}).encode()
250 
251         status, headers, body = self._gov.request(
252             login_url, method="POST", data=payload,
253             headers={"Content-Type": content_type},
254         )
255 
256         token = ""
257         if status in (200, 201) and body:
258             try:
259                 data  = json.loads(body)
260                 token = self._extract_json_path(data, token_path) or ""
261             except Exception:
262                 # Try regex fallback for non-standard responses
263                 m = re.search(r'"(?:token|access_token|jwt)"\s*:\s*"([^"]{20,})"', body)
264                 if m:
265                     token = m.group(1)
266 
267         if not token:
268             log.warning("JWT login failed (status=%d): could not extract token via path=%s",
269                         status, token_path)
270 
271         p = AuthProfile(
272             name=name, auth_type="jwt", token=token,
273             headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"} if token else {},
274             acquired_at=time.time(),
275         )
276         self._profiles[name] = p
277         return p
278

results.code:
278 
279     def cookie_capture(self, name: str, login_url: str,
280                        username: str, password: str,
281                        username_field: str = "username",
282                        password_field: str = "password") -> AuthProfile:
283         """
284         Perform login and capture Set-Cookie headers for session authentication.
285         """
286         payload = urllib.parse.urlencode({
287             username_field: username, password_field: password
288         }).encode()
289 
290         _, resp_headers, _ = self._gov.request(
291             login_url, method="POST", data=payload,
292             headers={"Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"},
293         )
294 
295         cookies: dict[str, str] = {}
296         for key, val in resp_headers.items():
297             if key.lower() == "set-cookie":
298                 m = re.match(r"([^=]+)=([^;]+)", val)
299                 if m:
300                     cookies[m.group(1).strip()] = m.group(2).strip()
301 
302         cookie_header = "; ".join(f"{k}={v}" for k, v in cookies.items())
303         p = AuthProfile(
304             name=name, auth_type="cookie",
305             cookies=cookies,
306             headers={"Cookie": cookie_header} if cookie_header else {},
307         )
308         self._profiles[name] = p
309         return p
310
results.issue_text: Possible hardcoded password: 'password'
